title: FailingEval
description: One unit of failure that a diagnosis tool consumes when the failure signal is an eval verdict. Adapters from Braintrust, LangSmith, OpenAI Evals, Inspect AI, promptfoo, deepeval, or in-house eval runners target this shape. agent-researcher's loader reads this as input; the spec lifts it from an implicit Python contract to a public schema so adapters can target it without reading source.

scenario_id.description: Stable identifier for the eval row within its source. Combined with experiment_id in metadata, must uniquely identify the failing case.

expected.description: The label or output the eval was looking for. Structured values (dicts, lists) are compact-JSON-encoded; bare strings pass through.

predicted.description: What the agent actually produced. Same encoding rule as `expected`.

predicted_confidence.description: The primary scorer's score on the predicted output (when scoring is numeric). Null when the scorer is boolean or the row has no primary scorer.

passed.description: Always false for records emitted by an adapter — this schema is for failures only. Present and explicit so the diagnostic agent doesn't have to infer.

score.description: Numeric score from the primary scorer. Mirrors predicted_confidence; both are kept for clarity.

scorer.description: Name of the primary scorer used to decide pass/fail for this row.

scorer_signature.description: Per-scorer pass/fail signature. Captures the pattern of which scorers a row failed, not just the primary verdict. The diagnostic agent reads this — fails-factuality-passes-exact-match is a different bug than fails-exact-match-passes-factuality.

spans.description: The agent's actual execution trace for this row, when the eval was instrumented. Adapters that source this from Braintrust, LangSmith, or an OTel-wrapped agent harness should populate it. Shape is opaque to the spec — pass through what the source provides. The single highest-leverage diagnostic input when present: without spans, the diagnoser reads source alone; with spans, it can localize a defect to the step of the agent's reasoning where it surfaced.

cluster_size.description: When a pre-clustering pass collapsed equivalent failures, the number of rows in this representative's cluster. Diagnostic agent uses this to prioritize: a cluster of 18 is a systemic defect; a cluster of 1 is scattershot.

cluster_member_ids.description: scenario_ids of every row this representative is standing in for. Audit trail for the clustering pass.

input.description: The eval scenario's input as the source platform recorded it. Free-form — the adapter passes it through.

output.description: Mirrors `predicted` but in the source platform's native shape (often structured rather than stringified).

notes.description: Human-readable summary of the failure context — scorer, score, threshold, source. Adapters generate this; agent-researcher renders it into the diagnostic prompt.

metadata.description: Adapter and source metadata. Conventional keys: experiment_id, experiment_name, project_name, row_id, agent_revision (git SHA the agent was at when the eval ran), tags. Adapters MUST set agent_revision when known — otherwise the diagnosis target may drift from the code that actually produced the failure.

agent_revision.description: Git SHA (or similar version identifier) for the agent source that produced this eval row. Required for trustworthy diagnosis — without it, the diagnoser may cite lines that have since moved.

created.description: ISO 8601 timestamp from the source platform.
